objective.description: Verify that the existing Celery configuration in api/tasks/document_processing_tasks.py meets enterprise standards and integrates properly with the Phase 2 changes (new entity extractor, chunker, retry logic).

context.description: Currently, the Celery app is configured within api/tasks/document_processing_tasks.py (lines ~197-228). There is no separate api/celery_config.py file.

actions.action: Ensure Redis configuration is properly loaded from api/config.py

prerequisites.prerequisite: Phase 2 completed (all services ported and integrated)

task.title: Audit Existing Celery Configuration
task.description: Check configuration elements including broker URL, result backend, task serializer, and result expires settings.

verification_command.command: cd AURA-NOTES-MANAGER ../../.venv/Scripts/python -c " import sys sys.path.insert(0, 'api') from tasks.document_processing_tasks import app print('Celery App Configuration:') print(f' Broker: {app.conf.broker_url}') print(f' Backend: {app.conf.result_backend}') print(f' Task serializer: {app.conf.task_serializer}') print(f' Result expires: {app.conf.result_expires}s') print(f' Task routes: {app.conf.task_routes}') "
verification_command.expected_output: Celery App Configuration: Broker: redis://127.0.0.1:6379/0 Backend: redis://127.0.0.1:6379/0 Task serializer: json Result expires: 3600s Task routes: {'api.tasks.*': {'queue': 'kg_processing'}}

task.title: Verify Redis Configuration Integration
task.description: Check that Redis config is loaded from api/config.py with proper environment variables.

verification_command.command:
# Verify config.py has Redis settings grep -n "REDIS" api/config.py
# Expected: # REDIS_HOST = os.getenv("REDIS_HOST", "127.0.0.1") # REDIS_PORT = int(os.getenv("REDIS_PORT", 6379)) # REDIS_DB = int(os.getenv("REDIS_DB", 0)) # REDIS_URL = f"redis://{REDIS_HOST}:{REDIS_PORT}/{REDIS_DB}"

if_missing.code_block: # Redis configuration REDIS_HOST = os.getenv("REDIS_HOST", "127.0.0.1") REDIS_PORT = int(os.getenv("REDIS_PORT", 6379)) REDIS_DB = int(os.getenv("REDIS_DB", 0)) REDIS_URL = f"redis://{REDIS_HOST}:{REDIS_PORT}/{REDIS_DB}" CELERY_RESULT_EXPIRES = int(os.getenv("CELERY_RESULT_EXPIRES", 3600))

task.title: Verify Celery Configuration Best Practices
task.description: Check Celery settings against best practices checklist.

task.title: Add Time Limits (Recommended)
task.rationale: Document processing can hang on large files or API timeouts. Time limits ensure tasks don't run indefinitely.
task.action: Add to Celery app configuration (after line 228):
task.code_block: # Time limits (5 minutes hard limit, 4 minutes soft warning) task_time_limit=300, # 5 minutes hard limit (raises SoftTimeLimitExceeded) task_soft_time_limit=240, # 4 minutes soft warning (allows cleanup)

verification_command.command:
# Test Redis connection redis-cli ping
# Expected: PONG
# If Redis not running (Windows with Chocolatey): # redis-server
# Or via Docker: # docker run -d -p 6379:6379 redis:alpine

template.markdown:
# Celery Configuration Documentation
## Overview Celery configuration for AURA-NOTES-MANAGER knowledge graph processing tasks.
## Configuration Location - **File:** `api/tasks/document_processing_tasks.py` (lines 197-228) - **Rationale:** Single-file configuration keeps task and config co-located
## Key Settings
| Setting | Value | Rationale | |---------|-------|-----------| | Broker | Redis (127.0.0.1:6379/0) | Fast, reliable message queue | | Result Backend | Redis (same instance) | Persistent result storage | | Serializer | JSON | Human-readable, secure | | Task Acks | Late (after completion) | Ensures re-queue on failure | | Prefetch | 1 task per worker | Prevents starvation on long tasks | | Result Expires | 3600s (1 hour) | Balance between visibility and storage | | Time Limit | 300s (5 min) | Prevents hanging tasks on large files |
## Queue Structure - **Default Queue:** `kg_processing` - **Route:** All `api.tasks.*` → `kg_processing` queue
## Environment Variables ```env REDIS_HOST=127.0.0.1 REDIS_PORT=6379 REDIS_DB=0 CELERY_RESULT_EXPIRES=3600 ```
## Worker Command ```bash celery -A api.tasks worker -l info -Q kg_processing -P solo --concurrency=2 ```
## Monitoring - Task states tracked in Redis (1-hour TTL) - Progress updates via `self.update_state()` - Firestore document status synced in real-time ```

command.code: ../../.venv/Scripts/python -c " from api.tasks.document_processing_tasks import app, ProcessingState, KGProcessingTask print('✅ All Celery imports successful') print(f' App name: {app.main}') print(f' Broker: {app.conf.broker_url}') "

../../.venv/Scripts/python -c " import redis from api.config import REDIS_HOST, REDIS_PORT, REDIS_DB r = redis.Redis(host=REDIS_HOST, port=REDIS_PORT, db=REDIS_DB) assert r.ping(), 'Redis not responding' print('✅ Redis connection OK') "

../../.venv/Scripts/python -c " from api.tasks.document_processing_tasks import app tasks = list(app.tasks.keys()) assert 'api.tasks.document_processing_tasks.process_document_task' in tasks print('✅ Tasks registered:') for t in tasks: if 'document_processing' in t: print(f' - {t}') "

next_steps.step: After completion, proceed to: 03-02-PLAN.md - Audit Document Processing Task

notes.note: No separate celery_config.py needed - current approach is acceptable
Time limits are recommended but not required for Phase 3 completion
Redis must be running for worker tests in Phase 3.3

verification_results.task: Celery configuration verified: - Broker: redis://127.0.0.1:6379/0 - Backend: redis://127.0.0.1:6379/0 - Task serializer: json - Result expires: 3600s - Task routes: {'api.tasks.*': {'queue': 'kg_processing'}}
Redis config confirmed in api/config.py: - Line 65: REDIS_URL defined - Line 68: CELERY_RESULT_EXPIRES defined
All 10 best practice settings verified in code. Time limits already set per-task (exceeds recommendation).
Time limits already configured in task decorators: - process_document_task: 1800s/1500s (hard/soft) - process_batch_task: 3600s/3000s (hard/soft)
Redis not running during verification - expected behavior. Note: Start Redis before running Celery workers.
Created: api/tasks/CELERY_CONFIG.md Comprehensive documentation with all settings.

notes.note: All Celery configuration meets or exceeds enterprise standards
Time limits were already implemented (better than plan requirement)
Redis connectivity requires starting Redis server first
FIX APPLIED: Updated document_processing_tasks.py to import REDIS_URL and CELERY_RESULT_EXPIRES from api/config.py instead of duplicating env var logic (review feedback 2026-01-28)
